codeReview.summary: The fix commit resolves the prior success-path gap by asserting measurable cursor advancement delta and version increments after refreshAll(). However, the failure-path test still does not prove that the persistence-error guard prevented runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded from running; unchanged cursor state can also occur when backfill is attempted against a read-only store and the advance write itself fails.

issues.description: test_refreshAll_withPersistenceFailure_preventsCursorAdvance() asserts only unchanged cursor state after running refreshAll() on a read-only database, but it never asserts that persistenceError was actually triggered by dataStore.insert(allUsages) nor that backfill was skipped. Given refreshAll() gates backfill on `if persistenceError == nil` (UsageAggregator.swift:242), this test can still pass if runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded() runs and fails to write cursor changes due to read-only mode, so the required guard behavior remains unproven.

sharedStateObservations.observation: token-accounting-worker procedure adherence evidence is still inconsistent with followedProcedure=true in this fix session.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The skill requires reading mission.md, AGENTS.md, .factory/library/architecture.md, and .factory/library/user-testing.md, but transcript skeleton evidence shows reads for mission/AGENTS/architecture only (worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton lines matching mission.md, AGENTS.md, architecture.md) and no user-testing.md read evidence; handoff verification.commandsRun only lists xcodebuild test.

summary: Fail: the prior success-path blocker is fixed, but the failure-path test still does not conclusively demonstrate that refreshAll() skipped scheduled backfill due to persistence error.
